The delegated task packet already provides:
- database_id: "dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37"
- date: "2026-03-28" (confirmed as tomorrow from the context)
- time: "10:00" (already specified)
- title: "台北看房"
- location: "台北"

I need to use the Notion MCP tools to add a row to this database. First, I should list the available tools to see what tool names are available for creating/updating database rows.

Let me call tools/list first to see what's available.</think>

tools.name: API-get-user
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a user
Error Responses:
400: 400

tools.name: API-get-users
tools.description:
Notion | List all users
Error Responses:
400: 400

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided. If not supplied, this endpoint will return the first page of results.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list desired in the response. Maximum: 100

tools.name: API-get-self
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve your token's bot user
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

tools.name: API-post-search
tools.description:
Notion | Search by title
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

query.description: The text that the API compares page and database titles against.

sort.description: A set of criteria, `direction` and `timestamp` keys, that orders the results. The **only** supported timestamp value is `"last_edited_time"`. Supported `direction` values are `"ascending"` and `"descending"`. If `sort` is not provided, then the most recently edited results are returned first.

direction.description: The direction to sort. Possible values include `ascending` and `descending`.

timestamp.description: The name of the timestamp to sort against. Possible values include `last_edited_time`.

filter.description: A set of criteria, `value` and `property` keys, that limits the results to either only pages or only data sources. Possible `value` values are `"page"` or `"data_source"`. The only supported `property` value is `"object"`.

value.description: The value of the property to filter the results by.  Possible values for object type include `page` or `data_source`.  **Limitation**: Currently the only filter allowed is `object` which will filter by type of object (either `page` or `data_source`)

property.description: The name of the property to filter by. Currently the only property you can filter by is the object type.  Possible values include `object`.   Limitation: Currently the only filter allowed is `object` which will filter by type of object (either `page` or `data_source`)

start_cursor.description: A `cursor` value returned in a previous response that If supplied, limits the response to results starting after the `cursor`. If not supplied, then the first page of results is returned. Refer to [pagination](https://developers.notion.com/reference/intro#pagination) for more details.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to include in the response. Maximum: `100`.

tools.name: API-get-block-children
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve block children
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided. If not supplied, this endpoint will return the first page of results.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list desired in the response. Maximum: 100

tools.name: API-patch-block-children
tools.description:
Notion | Append block children
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

block_id.description: Identifier for a [block](ref:block). Also accepts a [page](ref:page) ID.

children.description: Child content to append to a container block as an array of [block objects](ref:block)

after.description: The ID of the existing block that the new block should be appended after.

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-block
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a block
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

tools.name: API-update-a-block
tools.description:
Notion | Update a block
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

type.description: The [block object `type`](ref:block#block-object-keys) value with the properties to be updated. Currently only `text` (for supported block types) and `checked` (for `to_do` blocks) fields can be updated.

archived.description: Set to true to archive (delete) a block. Set to false to un-archive (restore) a block.

tools.name: API-delete-a-block
tools.description:
Notion | Delete a block
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-page
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a page
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

filter_properties.description: A list of page property value IDs associated with the page. Use this param to limit the response to a specific page property value or values. To retrieve multiple properties, specify each page property ID. For example: `?filter_properties=iAk8&filter_properties=b7dh`.

tools.name: API-patch-page
tools.description:
Notion | Update page properties
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

page_id.description: The identifier for the Notion page to be updated.

properties.description: The property values to update for the page. The keys are the names or IDs of the property and the values are property values. If a page property ID is not included, then it is not changed.

in_trash.description: Set to true to delete a block. Set to false to restore a block.

icon.description: A page icon for the page. Supported types are [external file object](https://developers.notion.com/reference/file-object) or [emoji object](https://developers.notion.com/reference/emoji-object).

cover.description: A cover image for the page. Only [external file objects](https://developers.notion.com/reference/file-object) are supported.

tools.name: API-post-page
tools.description:
Notion | Create a page
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

properties.description: The property values for the new page. The keys are the names or IDs of the property and the values are property values.

children.description: The content to be rendered on the new page, represented as an array of [block objects](https://developers.notion.com/reference/block).

icon.description: The icon of the new page. Either an [emoji object](https://developers.notion.com/reference/emoji-object) or an [external file object](https://developers.notion.com/reference/file-object)..

cover.description: The cover image of the new page, represented as a [file object](https://developers.notion.com/reference/file-object).

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-page-property
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a page property item
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

page_size.description: For paginated properties. The max number of property item objects on a page. The default size is 100

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-comment
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve comments
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

block_id.description: Identifier for a Notion block or page

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided. If not supplied, this endpoint will return the first page of results.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list desired in the response. Maximum: 100

tools.name: API-create-a-comment
tools.description:
Notion | Create comment
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

parent.description: The page that contains the comment

tools.name: API-query-data-source
tools.description:
Notion | Query a data source
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

data_source_id.description: Identifier for a Notion data source (database)

filter_properties.description: A list of page property value IDs to limit the response

filter.description: Filter conditions for querying the data source

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-data-source
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a data source
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

data_source_id.description: Identifier for a Notion data source

tools.name: API-update-a-data-source
tools.description:
Notion | Update a data source
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

data_source_id.description: Identifier for a Notion data source

tools.name: API-create-a-data-source
tools.description:
Notion | Create a data source
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

tools.name: API-list-data-source-templates
tools.description:
Notion | List templates in a data source
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

data_source_id.description: Identifier for a Notion data source

tools.name: API-retrieve-a-database
tools.description:
Notion | Retrieve a database
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

tools.name: API-move-page
tools.description:
Notion | Move a page
Error Responses:
400: Bad request

payload.raw_response:
<think>The user wants to add a new entry to their Notion calendar/database with these details:
- Date: 2026-03-28 (tomorrow)
- Time: 10:00 AM
- Title: 台北看房
- Location: 台北

Looking at the available tools, I can see `API-post-page` which is used to create a page. Since this is a database entry, I need to use the database parent type.

The database_id from context is: "dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37"

For Notion database entries, the properties need to be formatted according to Notion's schema. The user provided:
- date: "2026-03-28"
- time: "10:00"
- title: "台北看房"
- location: "台北"

Since this is a calendar/database, I need to check what properties it has. However, since the user is asking me to add an entry with specific date and time, I should assume these are datetime properties.

For Notion API, when creating a page in a database:
1. parent.type should be "database_id"
2. parent.database_id should be the database ID
3. properties should contain the property names/IDs as keys with their values

Let me construct the appropriate API call. I'll use `tools/call` with the `API-post-page` tool name since that's what's needed to create a new page (which will be a row in the database).

The arguments should include:
- parent: {type: "database_id", database_id: "dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37"}
- properties: {title: {"type": "title", "rich_text": [{"text": {"content": "台北看房"}}]}, date: {...}, location: {...}}

However, I don't know the exact property names/IDs for date and time. 

I should first retrieve the database to see its schema before adding a page with the correct property structure. This will let me match the user's requirements to the actual fields available in the calendar database.</think>
{"skill":"notion-basic","action":"tools/call","args":{"name":"API-retrieve-a-data-source","arguments":{"data_source_id":"dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37"}}}

payload.raw_response:
<think>The user wants to add an event to a Notion calendar database with the following details:
- Date: 2026-03-28 (tomorrow)
- Time: 10:00
- Title: "台北看房" (House viewing in Taipei)
- Location: 台北

The database ID provided is: dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37

However, the API-retrieve-a-data-source tool returned a 404 error saying it couldn't find the database with that ID. The error message suggests checking if the integration "claw_lite" has access to this database.

Since I cannot directly create a new page in a Notion database without knowing the exact property schema (property names and their types), and the retrieve tool failed, I need to:

1. Either ask the user for confirmation about the database ID or
2. Try to use a different approach like searching first

Looking at the available tools, I see there are API tools for creating data sources and pages, but typically in Notion you would create a new page (event) in an existing database. The issue is that we need to know:
- What properties exist in this database (name property, date property, datetime property, location property, etc.)

However, I notice the task packet already provides context with specific values for date, time, title, and location. This suggests these are expected properties.

Since the retrieve failed but we need to create a new row/page in this database, I should try using the API-create-a-data-source tool... wait, that's not right either. Looking at the tools available:

- API-retrieve-a-data-source
- API-update-a-data-source  
- API-create-a-data-source (this creates a new database, not a page)
- API-list-data-source-templates
- API-query-data-source (this queries/reads from a database)
- API-move-page


- Various comment and block tools

I don't see a direct "create page in database" tool. The typical Notion API for creating a page would be `pages/create` but I don't see that listed. Let me check if there's a search function or another way to find pages...

Looking at the available tools again, I see:
- Search and fetch tools (not explicitly named in this list but might exist)
- Page editing tools (create, update, move, duplicate, create comments)
- Database management tools

The error message indicates the database exists but the integration doesn't have access to it. Since I can't proceed without proper access to the database, I need to ask the user to verify the database ID or grant access to the integration. I don't see a direct "create page in database" tool in the available list. The tools available are mostly for reading (retrieve/query), updating data sources, or creating new data sources (databases themselves).

Since the retrieve failed and I can't find a create-page tool, I should ask the user if they have an alternative database ID or if this is a different integration issue that needs resolution first.</think>
The database with ID `dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37` cannot be found by the Notion integration "claw_lite". This could mean:

1. The database doesn't exist at that ID
2. The database exists but hasn't been shared with your Notion integration

Could you please:
- Verify the correct database ID for your calendar, or
- Ensure the database is shared with the integration "claw_lite" in your Notion workspace?

Once we have access to the database, I'll be able to create the event with:
- Title: 台北看房
- Date: 2026-03-28
- Time: 10:00
- Location: 台北

payload.raw_response:
<think>根據工具執行結果，Notion 資料庫 ID `dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37` 無法被 integration `claw_lite` 找到。這是因為：

1. 資料庫可能不存在於該 ID
2. 資料庫存在但尚未與 Notion integration 分享

我需要向 Admin 說明這個情況，並詢問正確的資料庫 ID 或確認資料庫已與 integration 分享。</think>
Notion 日曆資料庫無法存取，原因如下：

- 資料庫 ID `dca9bd99bf81412b99786996c72c5a37` 在 Notion integration "claw_lite" 中找不到
- 可能情況：資料庫不存在，或尚未與 integration 分享

請協助確認以下事項：

1. 提供正確的日曆資料庫 ID（可在 Notion 頁面右上角複製）
2. 或在 Notion workspace 中將該資料庫新增至 Integration "claw_lite" 的分享權限

確認後我再為您建立行程：台北看房，2026-03-28 10:00
